goal: Place a 1m cube centered at world coordinate (0, 0, 1) and a 0.5m-radius sphere centered at (2, 0, 1) on an empty Isaac Sim stage (Z-up meters). Verify placement with get_world_transform.
thoughts: Simple placement task; precision matters. Isaac Sim is Z-up meters by default. Use create_prim to place each primitive with an explicit xformOp:translate. Never use AddTranslateOp on a prim that already has xformOps — but fresh prims from create_prim don't, so a clean translate op is safe here. Verify with get_world_transform to confirm the translation took effect (catches the case where translate ops were silently dropped by a schema collision).
code:
# 1. Cube at (0, 0, 1), 1 m edge
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Cube",
    prim_type="Cube",
    translate=[0.0, 0.0, 1.0],
    attributes={"size": 1.0}
)

# 2. Sphere at (2, 0, 1), 0.5 m radius
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Sphere",
    prim_type="Sphere",
    translate=[2.0, 0.0, 1.0],
    attributes={"radius": 0.5}
)

# 3. Verify with world-space transforms
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/Cube")
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/Sphere")
failure_modes: Placing prims at origin and promising to move them later — does not satisfy the specific-coord task.
Y-up convention assumption — Isaac Sim is Z-up by default; (0, 1, 0) places the cube in the wrong axis.
Forgetting to verify with get_world_transform — translate ops can be silently overridden by parent xformOps if the prim is nested.
Setting size/radius wrong (e.g., 0.5 diameter instead of 0.5 radius for the sphere).
